name: email-handler
description: Create and send transactional emails using React Email. Covers templates, layout integration, and sending logic.
capabilities: Create React Email templates in src/emails/
Use Layout component for consistent styling
Integrate Tailwind CSS classes in email templates
Send emails using sendMail utility function
Render React components to HTML for email delivery
author: AayushBaniya2006
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only instructional content for creating React Email templates. All 78 static findings are false positives. The 'external_commands' patterns are markdown code fences used for documentation, not Ruby/shell execution. The 'network' references are documentation of how to use the sendMail function, not actual network calls. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are complete misclassifications since no cryptographic code exists. This skill generates template code only and poses no security risk.
valueStatement: Building responsive transactional emails from scratch is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill provides clear patterns and examples for creating professional HTML emails using React Email components and a consistent layout system.
userTitle: Create transactional emails with React Email
limitations: Requires React Email and sendMail to be set up in your project
Does not include email provider integration
Does not handle email delivery infrastructure
Templates must follow the specified directory structure

useCases.title: Build welcome emails

promptTemplates.title: Simple template request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a [email type] email template for [purpose]. Use the Layout component and include [specific elements like button, text, heading].

promptTemplates.title: Complete email flow
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a complete [email name] feature. Create the template in src/emails/ with props for [dynamic values]. Then add the sending logic to [API route or server action].

promptTemplates.title: Styled component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an email template with [specific styling requirements]. Use Tailwind classes for [styling goals]. Include [components like Button, Text, Link].

promptTemplates.title: Integration task
promptTemplates.prompt: Integrate [email type] sending into [function or API route]. The email should trigger when [condition] and include [dynamic data from your app].

bestPractices: Always include previewText in Layout for better email client previews
Define explicit TypeScript interfaces for email props
Use absolute URLs with environment variables for links
Match your app theme using bg-primary and text-foreground classes
antiPatterns: Hardcoding URLs instead of using appConfig or environment variables
Skipping the Layout component and losing consistent styling
Sending emails without error handling in the calling function

faq.question: Which React Email components are available?
faq.answer: Html, Button, Text, Heading, Link, Img, Hr, Container, and more from @react-email/components package.

faq.question: What email providers work with this skill?
faq.answer: Works with any provider supported by your sendMail implementation including Resend, SendGrid, Postmark, and AWS SES.

faq.question: How do I send emails in development?
faq.answer: Configure your sendMail function to use a local testing service or preview server during development.

faq.question: Is my email data sent to external services?
faq.answer: No. This skill only generates template code. Email sending is handled by your configured provider locally.

faq.question: Why are my emails not rendering correctly?
faq.answer: Ensure you wrap all content in the Layout component and use supported CSS properties compatible with email clients.

faq.question: How is this different from HTML email hand-coding?
faq.answer: React Email lets you build emails using familiar React components with automatic conversion to responsive HTML.
